summary: Full-stack backend+frontend testing of Afrobean ecommerce app. 44 backend pytest cases (43 passing after test fix). Critical frontend flows verified via Playwright: Home, Category, Collection, PDP + add-to-cart drawer, Meal collection, Cart, Login, Admin login → Dashboard, and end-to-end AI Assistant (GPT-5.2) → basket hydration → 'Add all to basket' → cart drawer with 8 items all working. One real backend bug found: /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 due to Stripe metadata validation in emergentintegrations library.

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error. Root cause: emergentintegrations/payments/stripe/checkout.py line 204 raises CheckoutError because `CheckoutStatusResponse.metadata` expects dict but Stripe returns a StripeObject. Error: '1 validation error for CheckoutStatusResponse metadata Input should be a valid dictionary [type=dict_type, input_value=<StripeObject...>]'. This BREAKS the CheckoutSuccess page polling — customers will never see payment confirmation.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend /checkout/success page polls GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} which currently returns 500 (see backend critical). User will see error or infinite loading.

action_items: Fix /api/checkout/status/{session_id} 500 error. The emergentintegrations StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status() fails Pydantic validation because Stripe returns session.metadata as a StripeObject, not a plain dict. Quick fix: wrap the call and catch CheckoutError, or normalise metadata before validation. Alternatively, call stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve directly and build the response manually with dict(session.metadata).
Note: this bug does NOT affect /api/checkout/session (creation) or the Stripe webhook handler, both of which work. Only the success-page polling path is broken.
critical_code_review_comments: routes_stripe.check_status does not handle CheckoutError — any exception from emergentintegrations bubbles up as a raw 500. Wrap in try/except and return a user-friendly 502/503.
routes_shop.py uses a lot of repeated `from auth import get_current_user; payload = await get_current_user(...)` boilerplate — could be factored into a helper dependency.
routes_stripe.py _compute_delivery_fee is duplicated logic with /api/delivery/quote (routes_shop). Consider extracting to a single util to avoid drift.
AI route /api/ai/chat has no rate limiting and sends full catalog context on every call (~120 products) — could become costly at scale.

Several admin endpoints accept `Dict[str, Any]` bodies without Pydantic validation (e.g. admin_update_product, admin_bulk_products, admin_adjust_stock) — validation is weak; missing fields cause silent issues.
routes_stripe.py `check_status`: if metadata is missing, falls back to txn.order_number which is safe, but the 500 path is not caught.
Admin delete endpoints return 200 even when the resource doesn't exist (no 404 on missing id).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@afrobean.co.uk / Admin@123 (verified). Customer: demo@afrobean.co.uk / Demo@123 (verified).
seed_data_creation: Test script creates disposable signup users (TEST_*@afrobean-test.co.uk) and one TEST product (then archives). OTP test uses example.com emails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_afrobean_api.py covers all public catalog, customer auth, OTP, cart (guest+auth), wishlist, AI chat + add-to-cart, admin CRUD, Stripe checkout creation, and checkout status. 43/44 passing — only failing test is Stripe status polling due to the emergentintegrations library bug. Frontend was tested via Playwright with data-testid selectors; AI flow uses [data-testid='ai-input']/[data-testid='ai-send']/[data-testid='ai-add-basket']. PDP uses [data-testid='add-to-cart-btn'] and opens a cart drawer with [role='dialog'].
rca_of_the_issue: Bug: /api/checkout/status returns 500. Reproduction: POST /api/checkout/session (creates Stripe session), immediately GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id}. Traceback: emergentintegrations/payments/stripe/checkout.py line 204 → CheckoutStatusResponse Pydantic model validation fails because Stripe SDK returns session.metadata as a `StripeObject` (dict-like but not a strict dict). Mitigation: either (a) upgrade/replace emergentintegrations with direct stripe SDK usage and cast metadata to dict(session.metadata), or (b) patch the returned object before passing to the Pydantic model. The /api/webhook/stripe path works and is the primary truth source for paid orders, so MVP-level payment flow is still recoverable via webhook, but the CheckoutSuccess page UX is broken.
